title: Zelenskyy Seeks Details on Putin's Proposed May 9 Victory Day Ceasefire in Ukraine
summary: Ukrainian President Zelenskyy instructed representatives to clarify details of Russia's proposed short-term ceasefire for May 9 Victory Day celebrations, while questioning whether the proposal covers 'a few hours of security for a parade in Moscow or something more.'
contents:
## Ukraine Ceasefire Diplomacy

### Zelenskyy's Response
Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy announced on Telegram that he instructed representatives to contact the US presidential team and clarify the details of Russia's proposed short-term ceasefire. He expressed skepticism about the scope: "We will find out exactly what the discussion covers — whether a few hours of security for a parade in Moscow or something more."

### Putin's Proposal
Russian President Vladimir Putin proposed a ceasefire for May 9 — when Russia celebrates Victory Day marking the defeat of Nazi Germany in World War II — during a phone call with President Trump on Wednesday. Kremlin spokesperson Dmitry Peskov clarified on Thursday that "no concrete decision has yet emerged" and that Putin alone would determine the specific terms.

### Ukraine's Counter-Position
Zelenskyy stated that Ukraine seeks a longer-term ceasefire rather than a brief holiday pause, indicating Kyiv views the May 9 proposal as insufficient without broader conflict resolution measures.

### Continued Combat Operations
Despite ceasefire discussions, combat operations continued unabated. Russian drone attacks killed one person in Dnipro and wounded dozens in Odesa, targeting residential buildings and civilian infrastructure. Ukrainian forces simultaneously struck Russian oil refineries in Perm and Orenburg and hit two Russian vessels in the Kerch Strait using sea drones.

### Grain Ship Diplomatic Victory
A ship carrying what Zelenskyy described as stolen Ukrainian grain departed Israel's Haifa port without unloading, after Israel's largest grain import company rejected the Russian-supplied shipment. Ukraine's Foreign Minister Sybiha called the outcome evidence that "Ukraine's legal and diplomatic actions have proven effective." Zelenskyy had threatened sanctions against Israel if the vessel discharged its cargo.
